critic_round1_raw: Second pass. Walk-forward cross-validation uses rolling 12-month training and 1-month evaluation fold within the training window. The final models are "retrained on the full 18-month training set using the best configurations." This retraining step is critical - does the best configuration from walk-forward CV (with 12-month training windows) generalize optimally when the full 18-month window is used? The hyperparameters optimized for 12-month windows may not be optimal for 18-month windows.
defender_round1_raw: The walk-forward CV with 12-month rolling windows is used for hyperparameter selection only, not for final model training. The final model is trained on all 18 months with the selected hyperparameters. This is the standard procedure for time series HPO: optimize hyperparameters on smaller rolling windows (more updates, faster), then train the final model on the full window. Hyperparameters like learning rate, regularization, and depth are generally transferable between similar-length windows. If there were a concern about window-length sensitivity, a 16-month training window HPO could be used instead, but this would leave fewer fold updates.
critic_round2_raw: Accepted - walk-forward CV for HPO followed by full-window retraining is standard practice. Hyperparameter sensitivity to window length is typically small for well-regularized models. No remaining concern. [Forced: no further methodological concern to add beyond what was addressed.]
adjudication_raw: Forced multiround run 2: Design confirmed sound. Verdict: defense_wins.
